title: Pipe clamp, pinch valve and method of use
abstract: A pipe clamp (20) for inhibiting fluid flow through a flexible pipe, comprising first (21) and second (22) contact members for positioning on opposing sides of a flexible pipe, and actuation means (29), optionally a pyrotechnically driven piston actuator, for automatically urging the contact members together, such that in-use a flexible pipe can be pinched closed. Particularly suited to inhibiting fluid flow in pipework under repair, or emergency shut off of fluid flow in failed pipework. Also relates to a pinch valve and method of use.
claims: Claims 1. A pipe clamp for inhibiting fluid flow through a flexible pipe, comprising first and second contact members for positioning on opposing sides of a flexible pipe, and actuation means for automatically urging the contact members together, such that in-use a flexible pipe can be pinched closed. 2. The pipe clamp of claim 1, wherein the actuation means is electrically initiated. 3. The pipe clamp of claim 2, further comprising a wireless receiver electrically connected to the actuation means. 4. The pipe clamp of any one of claims 1-3, wherein the actuation means comprises a pyrotechnically driven piston actuator. 5. The pipe clamp of any one of claims 1-4, wherein the actuation means comprises locking means for fixing the separation of the contact members. 6. The pipe clamp of any one of claims 1-5, wherein the first contact member is fixed within a housing, the second contact member being configured to slide within the housing towards the first contact member by action of the actuation means. 7. The pipe clamp of claim 6, further comprising means for clasping together the contact members at respective static ends, such that only a clamping end of the second contact member can slide within the housing. 8. The pipe clamp of claim 7, wherein the actuation means is positioned to act on the clamping end of the second contact member. 9. The pipe clamp of claim 6, wherein the housing comprises a pair of elongate apertures within which the second contact member is arranged to slide, such that the second contact member can slide towards the first contact member whilst remaining parallel thereto. 10. The pipe clamp of claim 9, wherein the actuation means is configured to act centrally upon the second contact member. 11. The pipe clamp of claim 10, wherein the actuation means comprises a pusher plate arranged to urge against the second contact member. 12. The pipe clamp of claim 11, wherein the actuation means comprises a propellant housing for holding a propellant, the propellant housing being arranged such that in use propellant gases can urge against the pusher plate. 13. The pipe clamp of claim 12, wherein the propellant housing is impact initiated. 14. The pipe clamp of any preceding claim, wherein the contact members are rods. 15. A pinch valve, comprising: a. the pipe clamp of any preceding claim; and b. a length of flexible pipe received between the contact members, and having an inlet end and an outlet end for coupling to pipework. 16. The pinch valve of claim 15 wherein the flexible pipe is formed from a resilient material. 17. The pinch valve of any one of claims 15-16, further comprising a valve housing. 18. A method of remotely inhibiting fluid flow in flexible pipe, comprising the steps of: a. Providing a pipe clamp comprising first and second contact members, and actuation means for automatically urging the contact members together; b. Positioning the first and second contact members on opposing sides of a flexible pipe; and then c. Triggering the actuation means, such that the flexible pipe is pinched closed.
